29.reason: [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. This causes EOF comparison to fail on systems where char is unsigned, leading to infinite loop or incorrect behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. When EOF (typically -1) is assigned to a char, it may become positive due to sign extension or truncation, causing the loop to never terminate or behave incorrectly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. This causes incorrect EOF comparison when char is signed, leading to premature loop termination or infinite loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. Comparing a char (which may be signed) with EOF (typically -1) can cause infinite loops if char is signed and byte 0xFF is read.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. This causes EOF comparison to fail on systems where char is unsigned, leading to infinite loop or incorrect behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [c is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. When char is signed, EOF (which is -1) may equal a valid character like 0xFF, causing premature loop exit or infinite loop.]

30.reason: [tolower expects an unsigned char cast to int or EOF. Passing a char that is negative (if plain char is signed) leads to undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [tolower expects an unsigned char cast to int or EOF. Passing a char that is negative (if plain char is signed) leads to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [tolower should only be applied to alphabetic characters; applying it to non-alphabetic (e.g., digits, punctuation) is unnecessary but not harmful. However, the bigger issue is that non-alphabetic characters are not skipped properly before starting a new word.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [tolower expects an unsigned char cast to int or EOF; passing a char that's negative (if plain char is signed) invokes undefined behavior.]

32.reason: [isalpha also expects an unsigned char cast to int or EOF. Same issue as above with negative char values.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [isalpha also expects an unsigned char cast to int or EOF. Same issue as above with negative char values.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [This inner loop assumes c is already alphabetic, but after reading a non-alphabetic character in the outer loop, c is converted to lowercase and then checked. However, if the first character read is non-alphabetic, this loop never runs, but w becomes empty string and is inserted into the list.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [isalpha also requires input to be representable as unsigned char or EOF; same issue as above.]

35.reason: [Same issue: c is char, but fgetc returns int. Also, if fgetc returns EOF here, it's converted to char and then passed to isalpha(), which is undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Same type mismatch: assigning int (from fgetc) to char c causes loss of information for EOF detection in inner loop.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Again, c is char, so EOF may be misinterpreted. Also, after reading EOF inside this loop, c becomes EOF (as char), and next isalpha(EOF) is undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.2 , reason:  [Same type issue: c is char, but fgetc returns int. Also, if n exceeds 19, buffer overflow occurs.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: c is char, but fgetc returns int. Also, if fgetc returns EOF here, it's converted to char and then passed to isalpha(), which is undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: c is char, but fgetc returns int. Also, if n exceeds 19, buffer overflow occurs because w[20] can hold max 19 letters + '\0'.]

38.reason: [Even if w is an empty string (n=0), it's passed to search and stored, leading to output of empty words.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Even if w is an empty string (n=0), it's passed to search and stored, leading to output of empty words.]
